title: Longest Common Subsequence
description:
Implement Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) using dynamic programming.

LCS finds the longest subsequence common to two sequences (not necessarily contiguous).

**Recurrence:**
```
dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1         if s[i-1] == t[j-1]
         = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])  otherwise
```

LCS length is used in ROUGE-L metric computation.

**Example:**
```
lcs('ABCBDAB', 'BDCAB') = 4  (BCAB or BDAB)
```
starter_code:
def lcs_length(s: list, t: list) -> int:
    """Compute Longest Common Subsequence length.

    Args:
        s: First sequence (list or string)
        t: Second sequence (list or string)

    Returns:
        Length of LCS as integer
    """
    pass
explanation: LCS is a classic DP problem used in diff tools (Unix diff), ROUGE-L computation, and bioinformatics (sequence alignment). Unlike edit distance, only matches contribute positively (no substitution cost). ROUGE-L uses LCS to measure sentence-level structural similarity without requiring contiguous matches, which is more flexible than bigram overlap (ROUGE-2).
hints: dp[i][j] represents LCS length of s[:i] and t[:j]
Otherwise: take max of skipping one char from either sequence
